title: Dynamic Rendering Decision and Isolated Browser-Service Boundary · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers dynamic rendering decision and isolated browser-service boundary into a changed least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through predicti…
objectives: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.
Define typed request, origin policy, viewport, actions, readiness, budgets, response, errors, trace, and cancellation for a separate controlled service.
Apply authorization, SSRF, resource type, download, WebSocket, worker, and byte rules to every browser-initiated request.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Render Necessity
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 2005 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove render necessity, typed browser port, subresource policy, untrusted results, and lifecycle evidence; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Render Necessity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Render Necessity, keep Render Necessity connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
